changes.value:
## What

Expose perspective listing to the frontend so perspectives can appear in the left nav alongside views. Currently the left nav renders views from `invoke("list_views")`. Perspectives need a similar pathway.

**`kanban-app/src/state.rs` (or equivalent Tauri command file):**
- Add `list_perspectives` Tauri command that calls `ListPerspectives` operation through dispatch
- Returns JSON array: `[{ id, name, view, ... }]` — enough metadata for the nav to render icons/labels
- Should be callable from frontend via `invoke("list_perspectives")`

**`kanban-app/src/state.rs` (events):**
- Emit `"perspectives-changed"` event when perspective mutations occur (add/update/delete)
- Frontend can listen and re-fetch perspective list reactively

**Consider:**
- Each perspective references a `view` (board or grid) — the nav could use the view's icon for the perspective
- Perspectives are separate from views in the nav (e.g., a "Perspectives" section below views, or inline with a distinguishing icon)
- The backend just needs to serve the list; layout decisions are frontend work (not this card)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `invoke("list_perspectives")` Tauri command exists and returns perspective array
- [ ] Perspective mutations emit `"perspectives-changed"` event
- [ ] Response includes `id`, `name`, `view` at minimum
- [ ] Works with empty board (returns empty array)
- [ ] Works after add/delete cycle

## Tests
- [ ] `test_list_perspectives_tauri_cmd` — invoke returns JSON array
- [ ] `test_perspectives_changed_event_on_add` — event emitted after add
- [ ] `test_perspectives_changed_event_on_delete` — event emitted after delete
- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p kanban-app perspectives`
PERSP-8: Perspective listing for left nav (Tauri command)

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,30 +1,35 @@
 ## What

-Expose perspective listing to the frontend so perspectives can appear in the left nav alongside views. Currently the left nav renders views from `invoke("list_views")`. Perspectives need a similar pathway.
+Expose perspective listing through the command system — NOT a Tauri command. Everything goes through command dispatch.

-**`kanban-app/src/state.rs` (or equivalent Tauri command file):**
-- Add `list_perspectives` Tauri command that calls `ListPerspectives` operation through dispatch
-- Returns JSON array: `[{ id, name, view, ... }]` — enough metadata for the nav to render icons/labels
-- Should be callable from frontend via `invoke("list_perspectives")`
+**Add to `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml`:**
+```yaml
+- id: perspective.list
+  name: List Perspectives
+  visible: false
+```

-**`kanban-app/src/state.rs` (events):**
-- Emit `"perspectives-changed"` event when perspective mutations occur (add/update/delete)
-- Frontend can listen and re-fetch perspective list reactively
+**Add to `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/perspective_commands.rs`:**
+- `ListPerspectivesCmd` — implements `Command` trait, delegates to `ListPerspectives` operation via dispatch
+- Returns JSON array of `{ id, name, view }` — enough for nav rendering

-**Consider:**
-- Each perspective references a `view` (board or grid) — the nav could use the view's icon for the perspective
-- Perspectives are separate from views in the nav (e.g., a "Perspectives" section below views, or inline with a distinguishing icon)
-- The backend just needs to serve the list; layout decisions are frontend work (not this card)
+**Register in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs`:**
+- Add `"perspective.list"` → `ListPerspectivesCmd` to `register_commands()`
+
+**Event emission:**
+- Perspective mutation commands (save, delete, filter, group, clearFilter, clearGroup) should emit a `"perspectives-changed"` event via the app handle after executing, so the frontend can re-invoke `perspective.list` reactively
+
+The frontend left nav will call `backendDispatch({ cmd: "perspective.list" })` and listen for `"perspectives-changed"` events to refresh — same pattern as views.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `invoke("list_perspectives")` Tauri command exists and returns perspective array
-- [ ] Perspective mutations emit `"perspectives-changed"` event
-- [ ] Response includes `id`, `name`, `view` at minimum
-- [ ] Works with empty board (returns empty array)
-- [ ] Works after add/delete cycle
+- [ ] `perspective.list` command registered and callable through dispatch
+- [ ] Returns JSON array with `id`, `name`, `view` per perspective
+- [ ] Returns empty array when no perspectives exist
+- [ ] Mutation commands emit `"perspectives-changed"` event
+- [ ] No Tauri `#[command]` — all through command system

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `test_list_perspectives_tauri_cmd` — invoke returns JSON array
-- [ ] `test_perspectives_changed_event_on_add` — event emitted after add
-- [ ] `test_perspectives_changed_event_on_delete` — event emitted after delete
-- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p kanban-app perspectives`
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] `test_list_perspectives_cmd_empty` — returns empty array
+- [ ] `test_list_perspectives_cmd_after_save` — returns saved perspective
+- [ ] `test_perspectives_changed_event` — verify event emission on mutations
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban commands::perspective`
\ No newline at end of file

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `perspective.list` command registered and callable through dispatch
-- [ ] Returns JSON array with `id`, `name`, `view` per perspective
-- [ ] Returns empty array when no perspectives exist
-- [ ] Mutation commands emit `"perspectives-changed"` event
-- [ ] No Tauri `#[command]` — all through command system

+- [ ] Response includes `id`, `name`, `view` at minimum
